2605.00257.title: Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning for Chartered Accountancy
2605.00257.paper_summary: The inception of Large Language Models (LLMs) has catalyzed AI adoption in the finance sector, yet their reliability in complex, jurisdiction-specific tasks like Indian Chartered Accountancy (CA) remains limited. The models display difficulty in executing numerical tasks which require multiple steps while also needing advanced knowledge about legal regulations and the method of scaling their operations is not feasible in settings which have limited access to resources. We present CA-ThinkFlow as a parameter-efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework which operates with a 14B, 4-bit-quantized reasoning model, 14B-DeepSeek-R1, and a layout-aware Docling extraction system which maintains document structure during extraction. CA-ThinkFlow uses a basic RAG method which automatically adds retrieved information into the prompt, while it depends on the model's built-in Chain-of-Thought (CoT) functions to create context and produce correct answers. The system we developed system operates at performance levels which match large proprietary models when we tested it on the multi-level CA-Ben benchmark, achieving Scholastic Reliability Coefficient (SRC) results which equal 68.75\% of GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet. The framework shows high efficiency and strength in handling parameters, but essential reasoning abilities fail to process complex regulatory texts which exist in fields such as Taxation.
2605.00257.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的出現促進了金融領域的人工智慧採用，但它們在複雜的、特定於法域的任務（如印度特許會計師（CA））中的可靠性仍然有限。這些模型在執行需要多步驟的數字任務時顯示出困難，同時也需要對法律法規有深入的了解，而在資源有限的環境中擴展其運作的方法並不可行。我們提出了CA-ThinkFlow，這是一個參數高效的檢索增強生成（RAG）框架，使用一個14B、4位量化的推理模型14B-DeepSeek-R1，以及一個在提取過程中保持文檔結構的佈局感知Docling提取系統。CA-ThinkFlow使用一種基本的RAG方法，自動將檢索到的信息添加到提示中，同時依賴模型內建的思維鏈（CoT）功能來創建上下文並產生正確答案。我們開發的系統在多層CA-Ben基準測試中表現出與大型專有模型相匹配的性能水平，實現了68.75\%的學術可靠性係數（SRC）結果，與GPT-4o和Claude 3.5 Sonnet相等。該框架在處理參數方面顯示出高效率和強大能力，但基本的推理能力無法處理存在於稅收等領域的複雜法規文本。
2605.00257.author: Jatin Gupta et.al.
2605.00257.authors: Jatin Gupta, Akhil Sharma, Saransh Singhania, Ali Imam Abidi
